sourceStatus.publicationPolicy: No long SEC source text is embedded; evidence uses short digests and source URL anchors.

summaryKo.oneLine: UPS는 최대 고객 물량 축소와 무역 정책 변화 속에서도 고수익 물량 중심의 전략적 전환을 지속하며 18억 2,200만 달러의 영업이익을 기록했습니다.

keyPoints.text: 최대 고객사와의 계약에 따라 2026년 6월까지 물량을 50% 이상 감축하는 전략적 물량 조정이 계획대로 진행 중입니다.
글로벌 무역 정책 변화와 관세 영향으로 중국-미국 노선 물량이 감소하며 국제 부문 마진에 압박을 받고 있습니다.
USPS와의 계약을 통한 항공 화물 매출 성장으로 소형 패키지 운영 부문의 수익성을 방어하고 있습니다.

riskChanges.text: 글로벌 무역 정책 및 관세 변화로 인한 특정 노선(중국-미국)의 물량 이동이 사업 운영 및 마진에 리스크로 작용하고 있습니다.
최대 고객사의 물량 이탈 가속화가 예상됨에 따라 향후 매출 및 물동량 변동성에 대한 관리 필요성이 증대되었습니다.

businessChanges.text: 운영 비용을 전년 동기 198억 7,400만 달러에서 193억 9,900만 달러로 절감하며 효율성 개선을 도모하고 있습니다.

financialHighlights.text: 순이익은 12억 8,300만 달러이며, 현금및현금성자산은 61억 9,400만 달러를 보유하고 있습니다.

watchItems.text: 최대 고객 물량 감소가 하반기 30% 수준으로 가속화될 예정이므로, 이를 상쇄할 고수익 SMB 및 헬스케어 부문의 성장 속도가 수익성 방어의 핵심입니다.

evidence.sourceTextDigest: Quarter ended 2025-06-30 revenue = 212억 2,100만 달러 (USD 21.221B; 21,221 million), XBRL range 2025-04-01..2025-06-30.
Quarter ended 2025-06-30 operating income = 18억 2,200만 달러 (USD 1.822B; 1,822 million), XBRL range 2025-04-01..2025-06-30.
Quarter ended 2025-06-30 net income = 12억 8,300만 달러 (USD 1.283B; 1,283 million), XBRL range 2025-03-31..2025-06-30. (quarter derived: YTD 2025-06-30 minus YTD 2025-03-31)
Quarter ended 2025-06-30 total assets = 709억 2,300만 달러 (USD 70.923B; 70,923 million), XBRL range instant..2025-06-30.
Quarter ended 2025-06-30 cash and cash equivalents = 61억 9,400만 달러 (USD 6.194B; 6,194 million), XBRL range instant..2025-06-30.

Management's Discussion and Analysis of Financial Condition and Results of Operations Overview We continue to execute our Customer First, People Led and Innovation Driven strategy to grow in the most attractive parts of the market including healthcare, small and medium-sized businesses ("SMBs") and International. During the second quarter and first half of 2025, we took several steps in furtherance of this strategy, including continuing our deliberate shift in our business to increase our focus on higher yielding volume. As previously disclosed, we have entered into an agreement with our largest customer that provides for reductions in the volume they ship with us, relative to 2024, by more than 50% by June 2026. Through the first half of 2025, this volume glide-down proceeded as planned, and in the second half 2025, we expect to accelerate the year-over-year pace of decline to close to

Overview We continue to execute our Customer First, People Led and Innovation Driven strategy to grow in the most attractive parts of the market including healthcare, small and medium-sized businesses ("SMBs") and International. During the second quarter and first half of 2025, we took several steps in furtherance of this strategy, including continuing our deliberate shift in our business to increase our focus on higher yielding volume. As previously disclosed, we have entered into an agreement with our largest customer that provides for reductions in the volume they ship with us, relative to 2024, by more than 50% by June 2026. Through the first half of 2025, this volume glide-down proceeded as planned, and in the second half 2025, we expect to accelerate the year-over-year pace of decline to close to 30%. Also as previously disclosed, in the first half of 2025 we insourced our former

Revenue in our global small package operations during the second quarter and year-to-date periods of 2025 benefited from growth in air cargo revenue as we fully onboarded volume under our contract with the U.S. Postal Service ("USPS") during the fourth quarter of 2024. Global trade policy changes including pending and enacted tariffs and the de minimis exclusions took effect during the second quarter of 2025 and resulted in shifting trade lane volumes, particularly reducing volumes on our China to U.S. lane, pressuring our International segment margins during the second quarter. Partially offsetting this decline was growth on other trade lanes, including from China to the rest of the world and the rest of the world into the U.S. Supply Chain Solutions revenue decreased in both the second quarter and year-to-date periods of 2025, driven by the impact of the third quarter 2024 divestiture
